skill.name: prompt-engineering
skill.description: Expert guide on prompt engineering patterns, best practices, and optimization techniques. Use when user wants to improve prompts, learn prompting strategies, or debug agent behavior.
skill.summary: Master prompt engineering with proven patterns for few-shot learning, chain-of-thought reasoning, and systematic prompt optimization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation-only resource about prompt engineering techniques. The 'external_commands' patterns appear in markdown code examples (not executable code), and no cryptographic code exists in the file. The skill contains educational content about prompting strategies with no security risks.

content.value_statement: Struggling with inconsistent AI outputs and unreliable responses. Learn proven prompt engineering patterns that maximize LLM performance, reliability, and controllability.
actual_capabilities: Teaches few-shot learning techniques with 2-5 input-output examples for consistent formatting
Explains chain-of-thought prompting to improve accuracy on analytical tasks by 30-50%
Provides systematic prompt optimization methods through A/B testing and iteration
Shows how to build reusable prompt template systems with variables and conditionals
Guides effective system prompt design for role-based and persistent behavior
limitations: This skill provides educational guidance only - it does not execute or run prompts
Techniques require access to Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to apply in practice
Results may vary depending on the specific LLM model and version used
Token costs increase with more examples and longer reasoning chains

use_cases.title: AI Developer Improving Code Generation
use_cases.description: Backend developers can learn prompt patterns to generate more accurate code reviews, API designs, and documentation from AI assistants.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using AI coding assistants

use_cases.title: Content Creator Optimizing AI Outputs
use_cases.description: Writers and marketers can apply few-shot learning and template systems to produce consistent, on-brand content at scale.

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Structuring AI Analysis
use_cases.description: Analysts can use chain-of-thought prompting to verify AI reasoning on complex data problems and reduce errors in analytical tasks.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and business intelligence professionals

prompt_templates.title: Basic: Direct Instruction
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this article in 3 bullet points, focusing on the key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple tasks where basic constraints improve output quality

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Few-Shot Example Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Tasks requiring consistent formatting and handling of edge cases

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this bug report and determine root cause.

Think step by step:
1. What is the expected behavior?
2. What is the actual behavior?
3. What changed recently that could cause this?
4. What components are involved?
5. What is the most likely root cause?

Bug: "Users can't save drafts after the cache update deployed yesterday"
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex problems requiring multi-step logic and verifiable reasoning

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Template System with Variables
prompt_templates.scenario: Reusable patterns for multi-turn conversations or repeated similar tasks

output_examples.input: User asks: How do I improve my prompts for code review tasks?
output: Use a structured template with context, code block, and specific focus areas
Include 2-3 example reviews showing the depth and format you expect
Request step-by-step analysis before final recommendations
Specify output format: Analysis, Recommendation, Code Example, Trade-offs

output_examples.input: User wants consistent JSON output from AI for ticket classification
output: Provide 3-5 input-output example pairs showing different ticket types
Include edge cases like missing information or ambiguous requests
Show the exact JSON schema with null values for missing fields
End with "Now process:" followed by the new ticket to classify

best_practices: Start simple and iterate - begin with direct instructions, add complexity only when needed based on observed results
Show examples instead of describing rules - 2-5 input-output pairs consistently outperform verbose explanations
Test extensively on diverse inputs including edge cases before deploying prompts to production workflows
anti_patterns: Over-engineering prompts with complex structures before trying simple direct instructions first
Using examples that do not match the target task, causing confused or inconsistent model behavior
Exceeding token limits with excessive examples that push out relevant context from the prompt window

faq.question: How many examples should I include for few-shot learning?
faq.answer: Start with 2-5 input-output pairs. More examples improve accuracy but consume tokens. Balance based on task complexity and your token budget.

faq.question: What is chain-of-thought prompting and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Chain-of-thought asks the model to show step-by-step reasoning before the final answer. Use it for complex problems, mathematical reasoning, or when you need to verify the model's logic. It can improve accuracy by 30-50% on analytical tasks.

faq.question: How do I know if my prompt needs optimization?
faq.answer: Test on diverse inputs and measure accuracy, consistency, and token usage. If results vary significantly or miss key requirements, systematic A/B testing of prompt variations can identify improvements.

faq.question: What is the difference between system prompts and user prompts?
faq.answer: System prompts set global behavior, role, and constraints that persist across the conversation. User prompts contain variable content and specific instructions for each turn. Use system prompts for stable instructions.

faq.question: Can I use these techniques with any AI model?
faq.answer: Yes, these patterns work across Claude, Codex, and other LLMs. However, effectiveness varies by model. Test and adjust based on the specific model you are using.

faq.question: How do I handle edge cases and errors in prompts?
faq.answer: Include fallback instructions, request confidence scores, and specify how to indicate missing information. Ask for alternative interpretations when the model is uncertain about the input.
